summary: Soundboard capture is a little muddy early on, but the show shines with a driving Bertha, soaring China Cat > I Know You Rider and a fiery Goin' Down the Road Feeling Bad.
review: The performance is typical of a fall ’72 set – an average first half that really gels in the second, delivering standout moments like Bertha, China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Sugar Magnolia and a superb Goin' Down the Road. The SBD source suffers from early mud, channel imbalances and occasional mic glitches, but later sections gain clarity and good instrument separation.

ai_rating.rationale: Only one detailed review; it rates the show around 3½–4 stars and notes both recording flaws and strong musical moments, leading to a mid‑high overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Early set is muddy with missing drum channel and mic dropouts (Deal, El Paso). Later the mix improves; mics are high on Box of Rain, some channel reversal between sources, but overall separation is decent.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Goin' Down the Road Feeling Bad
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set feels average and a bit thin, but the second set picks up momentum with tighter playing and extended jams, resulting in a concise 1 hr 20‑min show that still delivers several high points.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Not at peak form early (mic out on Deal, curt leads), overuses pinch harmonics on Sugar Magnolia, but later shows great reverb tone and an amazing second solo on Goin' Down the Road.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mostly buried in one channel early; later comes forward on Box of Rain with decent performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Solid throughout; holds his squawks to the end of Sugar Magnolia and adds strong backing vocals.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy (Bill Kreutzmann) solid but drums are missing or low in first set, gradually become more audible later.
